channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Ghost Recon Project OVR Scope Cut To Meet New Deadlines
item.content:encoded:
Things continue to change with the next mainline Ghost Recon game at Ubisoft. After exclusively reporting that the new Ghost Recon title, known internally as Project OVR, was struggling due to what those working on the game called “unrealistic deadlines and poor planning and management”, Insider Gaming has learned more about what’s happened in recent months.

Since the previously reported memo was sent to the team in mid-April, Ubisoft has held what were described as “silent layoffs” at its Paris location.

Things Aren’t Going Well For Ghost Recon

VP of Production Jean-Baptiste Duval and VP of the Global Creative Office Julien Sansalone, who were brought in to be more present with the game’s development, are said to be acting as overseers on the project. Their presence, I was told, hasn’t changed the general day-to-day work, aside from layoffs.

Sources who spoke under the condition of anonymity said that the scope of the game “has been cut and greatly reduced, with many features stripped from the project”. Though specific features weren’t mentioned, it was said that they were a mix in terms of size.

Earlier this spring, Project OVR failed its Alpha test, leading to major changes on the project. Sources tell me that it wasn’t surprising the game failed alpha, with many saying that the game is in a “terrible state” and “isn’t stable at all.”

Currently, Ubisoft is targeting November for Project OVR to enter beta. With a timeline like that, developers working on the game are worried that “long days of crunching are ahead”. And with the recent layoffs, it means more work for those remaining at the studio.

Ubisoft has not responded to requests for comment.

What do you think of what’s going on with Project OVR and the Ghost Recon franchise? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, or join the discussion in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about what one EA executive said about the use of AI in game development. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: T1 Worlds 2025 Winner Skins Revealed and Every Easter Egg Confirmed
item.content:encoded:
The T1 Worlds 2025 Skins have finally been revealed following the LCK team’s historic triumph over KT Rolster last year.

T1’s 2025 roster will have their own skins in League of Legends for Ambessa, Xin Zhao, Galio, Miss Fortune, Yunara, and Seraphine and will earn a believed 30% share from the skin’s profits.

This was a third consecutive Worlds win for Lee ‘Faker’ Sang-hyeok, Mun ‘Oner’ Hyeon-jun, Ryu ‘Keria’ Min-seok, and now-Hanwha Life Esports pro Lee ‘Gumayusi’ Min-hyeong.

Meanwhile, it was a record sixth Worlds win for Faker and T1 overall and a first for toplaner Choi ‘Doran’ Hyeon-joon.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

When Will the T1 Worlds 2025 Skins Release?

The T1 Worlds 2025 skins have been confirmed to release with LoL Patch 26.14, which is due to release on July 16.

The Worlds skins are releasing earlier this year to make room for the third LoL Hall of Legends skins to release around Worlds 2026.

  • Fancy yourself an esports expert? Start betting with a $20 deposit at Polymarket and receive a $50 bonus!

T1 Worlds 2025 Ward Skin

As well as the six champion skins, there will also be a new “Magical Cat Strategist” ward skin in tribute to Im ‘Tom’ Jae-hyeon.

The ward skin unofficially bears a clear resemblance to Tom from the Tom and Jerry cartoon series as well as coach Tom’s haircut.

The skin also features Dr Mundo on the pages of his notebook in reference to coach Tom telling Oner to first-time Mundo on-stage at Worlds 2025.

This is the fourth T1 ward skin, with another having been made for coach Kim ‘kkOma’ Jeong-gyun.

Tom is still acting as interim head coach for T1 as Kkoma takes a temporary break.

T1 Worlds 2025 Nexus Explosion

There will also be a T1-themed Nexus explosion to customise players’ own victory screens.

As well as T1 visuals, it also captures the atmosphere of esports matches with the sounds of enthusiastic cheers from a crowd.

T1 Worlds 2025 Skins Easter Eggs

As revealed in a report from Naver, the linking idea between these skins is “Ascended Warriors,” embodying a warrior reaching the level of a god after T1’s historic victory.

Riot’s three core principles which led the designs for this skin line were: Legacy, Hierarchy, and Natural Style.

Doran – Ambessa

For his first Worlds skin, Doran asked Riot to give Ambessa a hairstyle reminiscent of a lion’s mane, dark-colored attire, and a strong red glow on her weapon.

Due to the hairstyle, the skin has a unique silhouette compared to base-Ambessa and therefore has new animations.

Doran also asked for Ambessa to be made to look younger, but this request was denied to preserve her identity.

As for references, there a ton during T1 Ambessa’s recall, including a Krug in reference to Doran imfamsouly dying to the jungle camp in the LCK last year.

Ambessa also picks up Doran’s Ring, Doran’s Shield, and Doran’s Blade in-game items in reference to the toplaner’s gamertag.

Oner – Xin Zhao

Oner’s requests were supposedly the simplest as he just wanted a red weapon and a jacket-like cape, as Oner typically wears his T1 uniform.

The recall features a tiger head similar to the necklace/pendant Oner regularly wears.

And the recalls also features a Lee Sin kick, a Poppy hammer, and Pantheon’s shield in tribute to previous victories he has had with the team.

Faker – Galio

Faker finally has a skin for Galio, one of his many signature champions.

To create the skin, Riot had to remove Galio’s existing split-wing and create a new, unique, one-piece wing that resembles the T1 logo.

During Galio’s W, a move that taunts all around him, the Demacian character does Faker’s iconic shh pose.

As for the recall animation, the six stars are obviously in reference to Faker’s six Worlds wins.

The recall also sees the Earth shrink under Faker as he ascends to god status and the VFX was made larger upon the player’s request.

Gumayusi – Yunara

One of the reasons Gumayusi chose Yunara is that the champion’s original designer made her with the pro ADC’s playstyle in mind.

The skin gives Yunara a ‘twin bun’ hairstyle at the player’s request, and T1’s mascot, ‘Arti,’ is inside the weapon spheres.

The recall features a musical sequence made for the skin while Yunara meditates with birds tweeting around her.

Gumayusi had a very difficult 2025 and was benched at one point, but eventually regained his place and won the Worlds MVP award.

The recall tells that story. During the meditation, Yunara faces some internal strife and conflict, but manages to regain composure as a butterfly lands on her.

This is also a reference to when a butterfly flew behind Gumayusi once on stage and was seen as a good luck symbol.

Keria – Seraphine

Keria wanted his Seraphine skin to exude beauty and cuteness, and enhanced the overall quality of her base model by requesting delicate details.

One such tiny detail is that Seraphine’s signature tattoo on her cheek is in a ‘K’ shape inspired by his initial.

The recall animation sees Seraphine performing a show in front of a large LED screen stage while wearing a T1 uniform.

And it pays homage to the 2025 winning moment with multiple faces that match close-up shots of Keria’s expressions during the livestream of T1’s victory.

Gumayusi – Miss Fortune Prestige

The prestige mythic Miss Fortune skin Gumayusi received for earning Worlds MVP has a lot of unique details to it.

The skin has unique animations and will have a new voice-over; the English version is expected to be recorded by Laura Bailey again.

The development team specially created three different versions of her weapons, which represent the three championship-winning years (23, 24, and 25) Gumayusi had on T1. 

Whenever T1 MF ults, she switches to a different set of weapons and features a unique wind-blown hairstyle animation.

Additionally, a Baron doll appears during one animation in reference to Gumayusi’s famous blind Baron steal during Worlds 2022.

The recall animation features an appearance from his beloved dog Doongi and reflects the hardships ‘Gumayusi’ has fought against throughout his career.

During it, he dodges Kalista’s spear like in the 2023 WBG match, dodges Ashe’s arrow in reference to the 2024 BLG match, and shoots down Ziggs’ bomb like in the past KT match.

T1 Miss Fortune then ultimately heroically stands tall and grabs the MVP trophy.

Which of these T1 skins is your favourite? Share your opinions with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

Additionally, see how to build Locke and tips for playing the new LoL champion.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 New Trailer Provides First Look at Gameplay
item.content:encoded:
A brand new gameplay trailer has arrived for Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3, providing players with a first look at West City, all the different moves players can pull off, the new features, and more.

Players have been waiting for the first look at the title’s gameplay ever since Bandai Namco confirmed that Dragon Ball Age 1000 was indeed Xenoverse 3. We already know that the title is going to arrive for PlayStation 5, XBOX Series X|S, and PC via Steam in 2027, and now players know what to expect.

Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 Gameplay Trailer Provides First Look at New Feature

The new gameplay trailer for Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 begins with Shenron’s voice inviting players to embark on an exciting new adventure. The trailer showcases West City, highlighting various characters players will interact with, the stunning cityscapes, and the locations available for exploration. Additionally, we dive straight into gameplay, offering players their first glimpse of the intense combat they will experience.

MORE NEWS | Synchronized Dragon Ball FighterZ Moment Goes Viral as FGC Legends Go in Different Directions

Players also gain a basic understanding of the narrative, which reveals that they will need to enroll in a school to learn the necessary skills. There, they will encounter additional characters who will play significant roles in the story. As part of a squad, players will embark on missions and can take on enemies with their team’s help. The storyline definitely appears to be original.

The gameplay trailer for Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 introduces an exciting new feature. Players can now push a blue flame inside their character to summon their inner Trunks, gaining the ability to perform all of Trunks’s signature moves. Additionally, the trailer reveals that players can summon their inner Piccolo, Krillin, Tien, Vegeta, and more, allowing them to use these characters’ signature moves.

Towards the end of the video, players get a brief look at the transformations, featuring the player character’s ability to become Super Saiyan, use Kaioken, and more. You can check out the new gameplay trailer for Xenoverse 3 below:

What are your thoughts on the new Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 gameplay? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Capcom seemingly not interested in Resident Evil 5 and 6 remakes. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: All Steam Machine Specs & Comparisons—Steam Machine Vs PS5 Pro
item.content:encoded:
When it comes to new consoles and PCs, specifications are usually one of the first things people research, and for good reason! How powerful the system is will determine how well it can run various games, and at the end of the day, that’s what matters most.

Well, the latest to fall under the microscope is the Steam Machine, Valve’s latest attempt at bridging the PC and console market. Sure, it can be used to kick back on the sofa and play your Steam library, but it can also be utilised as a functioning PC.

With such varying uses and a hefty price tag, people are naturally wondering how the Steam Machine compares to other similar systems. So, without further ado, here’s the full list of Steam Machine specs, as well as how it compares to the likes of the PlayStation 5 Pro—the strongest console on the market.

All Steam Machine Specs

From its GPU to RAM, CPU to Storage, there’s plenty to unpack when looking into what makes the Steam Machine tick.

The premise of the Steam Machine is to be a functioning gaming PC in a small package, and judging by its performance and specs, it does exactly what it says on the tin. It’s not a NASA supercomputer worthy of running games at the highest possible framerates, but it does the job of being an easy entry point for potential PC gamers—or for those looking to take gaming away from a desktop.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Buy Steam Machine—Release Date & Pre-Order Purchase Guide

You’ll find the full specification table below, encompassing its general specs, software, connectivity and everything in between:

Steam Machine Vs PS5 Pro

For those of you looking to use the Steam Machine for more of a console purpose, you’ll likely want to compare the new system to the PlayStation 5 Pro.

The PS5 Pro is widely considered the most powerful console on the market right now, with PSSR (PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution) and Advanced Ray Tracing offering an optimised and immersive gaming experience. 120fps with near 4K output is obtainable with compatible games, and there’s no denying it’s a titan to compete with, but can the Steam Machine compare?

  • More Insider Gaming: How Much Does Steam Machine Cost? All US & UK Editions & Bundles Price Guide

Judging by the specs alone, it’s a tough match-up. Here’s how they line-up:

In some aspects, it’s tough to compare, especially since Valve lists some specs as ‘semi-custom.’ That said, when looking at raw performance, the PS5 Pro trumps the Steam Machine in practically every regard.

Of course, the Steam Machine has other capabilities, such as simply working as a makeshift PC, but if we’re just talking about its gaming potential, it’s no match for the PS5 Pro.

Considering that the Steam Machine costs over $400 more than the PS5 Pro, and the fact that GTA 6 won’t be playable on it at launch, it’s definitely a product aimed at the Steam faithful—at least you won’t have to pay for online multiplayer!

Thinking of buying one? Check out the trailer below one last time before taking the leap.

Will you be buying a Steam Machine at those prices? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more on the Steam Machine, read about how it was initially planned to be cheaper, and don’t forget to sign up for our Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: All Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Platforms—Can You Play on PlayStation, Xbox, PC, or Switch?
item.content:encoded:
It doesn’t matter if it’s a remake; Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is hot stuff, and Ubisoft would be wise to make it as available as possible across PlayStation, Xbox, Nintendo, and PC. Let me guide you through all of its platforms.

Last gen is still going strong, and current gen won’t be current-gen for much longer with the PS6 and Project Helix around the corner. Unless you have ‘Mario’ or ‘Zelda’ in the title, it’s never clear which games are on which platforms anymore.

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is a must-buy game in 2026, so let’s see where X marks the spot.

Can You Play Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced on PS5 or PS4?

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is releasing on modern PlayStation 5 consoles, but it won’t be following in the footsteps of the original Black Flag by coming out on PS4 as well.

It would have been poetic to get a Resynced port for PS4, but, logically, it would have cost Ubisoft a lot of time and money to optimize it enough to run on PS4 hardware, without compromising the PS5 build.

  • More Insider Gaming: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Release Date & Times

If you’re desperate to play it on PS4, you’ll need to dig out a PS4 copy (or even PS3!) and console. PS5 users are in luck.

Can You Play Black Flag Resynced on Xbox Series X|S or Xbox One?

An Xbox Series X|S release of Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is marked on the map, but there’s no sign of an Xbox One version for miles around.

Like the PS5, the Xbox gets a current-gen port, but Ubisoft has cut off any generations that have come before.

Can You Play Black Flag Remake on PC?

There’s usually never any doubt about PC ports, and that continues as Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced is definitely coming to PC at launch.

  • More Insider Gaming: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Trophy List

More questions need to be asked about Denuvo and how well it will run on PC, but the main thing has been asked and answered: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced can be purchased through Steam or the Ubisoft store.

Can You Play Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced on Nintendo Switch or Switch 2?

A Nintendo Switch or Switch 2 version hasn’t been given the green light, which means you won’t be playing Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced on any handheld Nintendo consoles, not legally anyway!

There’s plenty of AC games available on both Switch consoles, including the OG Black Flag, but Resynced isn’t coming to Switch 2 or its predecessor.

Check out the Summer Game Fest 2026 trailer to see how Resynced looks.

On a scale of 1-10, how excited are you for Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced? Let us know through the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, check out the report that the PS6 won’t be delayed to 2028 or 2029, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton
